items.criterion: Video-mode shot groups with an outdated videoAssetKey show an amber RefreshCw badge
Context menu on stale video-mode groups shows 'Update to Latest Video' action
Clicking 'Update to Latest Video' swaps the clip asset to the latest final video and clears the stale badge
Image-mode shot group sync behavior is completely unchanged
TypeScript compiles with no new errors
imageClipSnapshot is preserved when updating to latest video (switch-to-images still works after update)
Stale badge is hidden while a task is active on the group (consistent with clip-level stale badge)
No new hooks created — stale detection is a derived memo, update action lives in existing useSwitchToFinalVideo
Badge positioning and styling matches existing final-video and active-task badges
Manual smoke test: pin group → switch to video → generate new final video → badge appears after refresh → update works

unresolved_flags.concern: Final-video duration handling: Recurring debt: step 4 still swaps in a new final-video asset without any duration source, so a shorter regenerated final video can be asked to play for the old group span and overrun the real media length.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:23-37` still fetches only `id, location, thumbnail_url, shot_id, created_at` with no duration metadata. The existing final-video insertion path in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:137-153` also creates final-video assets with only `file/type/generationId` and sizes the clip from the timeline span. Playback then trims by `clip.to` in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:115-118`, so the proposed update-to-latest path inherits the same shorter-media overrun risk unless the plan adds a duration source or explicit containment.

unresolved_flags.concern: Final-video refresh path: the plan assumes `useShotFinalVideos()` refreshes every 30s, but in this repo it does not poll; stale detection only refreshes when some other invalidation fires. The badge/update flow is under-specified unless the implementation explicitly relies on the existing invalidation path or adds refetching.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:20-37` sets `staleTime: 30_000` but has no `refetchInterval`, so React Query will not auto-refresh every 30s. The actual refresh hook-up is the broader completed-task invalidation in `src/shared/hooks/useRealtimeInvalidation.ts:109-121`, which is different from the plan's stated polling assumption and manual verification step.

rationale: Light plan, 2 minor flags. Will address during execution: (1) test coverage for the new stale detection - will add. (2) updateToLatestVideo needs to update both the asset registry entry and the pinnedShotGroup.videoAssetKey atomically - will use pinnedShotGroupsOverride on the applyEdit.
signals_assessment: Forced proceed override applied by the orchestrator.
orchestrator_guidance: Force-proceed override applied. Proceed to finalize.

signals.idea:
Unify shot group sync into a single usePinnedGroupSync hook that handles both image and video mode updates.

## Current state
- Image sync: usePinnedGroupSync in usePinnedShotGroups.ts watches ShotsContext, auto-adds/removes clips when shot images change (for groups in mode:'images')
- Video staleness: useStaleVariants watches Supabase realtime + polls generations table for primary variant changes on individual clips. This is a separate system that doesn't know about shot groups or final videos.
- Final video data: useShotFinalVideos (called via useFinalVideoAvailable) fetches finalVideoMap from shot_final_videos view, refreshes every 30s

## What to build
Extend usePinnedGroupSync to also handle video mode:
- For groups in mode:'video': compare the group's videoAssetKey against finalVideoMap.get(shotId).id
- If they differ (newer final video exists), surface this as a 'stale video' state on the group
- Render a badge on the shot group (similar to the stale variant amber badge on clips) indicating 'New video available'
- Context menu gains 'Update to latest video' option that swaps the video clip's asset to the new final video
- This replaces the need for useStaleVariants to handle final-video-in-shot-group cases

## Data flow
- usePinnedGroupSync already receives: data, dataRef, applyEdit, shots, registerGenerationAsset
- Also needs: finalVideoMap (from useFinalVideoAvailable/useShotFinalVideos)
- Returns: existing sync behavior + staleShotGroupIds: Set<string> (shot groups with outdated video)

## Key files
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts - extend usePinnedGroupSync
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx - pass finalVideoMap to sync, wire stale state
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx - render stale video badge on groups
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ShotGroupContextMenu.tsx - add 'Update to latest video' action
- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts - may need an updateToLatestVideo function

unresolved_flags.concern: Final-video duration handling: Recurring debt: step 4 still swaps in a new final-video asset without any duration source, so a shorter regenerated final video can be asked to play for the old group span and overrun the real media length.

Final-video refresh path: the plan assumes `useShotFinalVideos()` refreshes every 30s, but in this repo it does not poll; stale detection only refreshes when some other invalidation fires. The badge/update flow is under-specified unless the implementation explicitly relies on the existing invalidation path or adds refetching.
